message:
test(core): port benchmarks and integration tests to runtime_compat abstractions

Migrate benchmarks and integration tests from direct tokio API usage to the
runtime_compat abstraction layer, enabling them to compile under both tokio
and asupersync feature flags.

benches/socket_throughput.rs:
- Replace tokio::io::{AsyncReadExt, AsyncWriteExt} with unix::{AsyncReadExt,
  AsyncWriteExt} from runtime_compat
- Replace tokio::net::UnixStream::pair() (not available in asupersync) with
  explicit bind + connect + accept via runtime_compat::unix helpers

benches/task_spawn.rs:
- Remove .expect() calls on spawn_bounded_with_cx() which now returns Vec
  directly instead of Result<Vec>

tests/asupersync_unix_socket.rs:
- Import AsyncReadExt/AsyncWriteExt from runtime_compat::unix instead of tokio
- Wrap timeout read_exact call in async block returning () to satisfy
  asupersync's stricter return type requirements

tests/egress_tap_integration.rs:
- Replace tokio::sync::{RwLock, mpsc} with runtime_compat::{RwLock, mpsc}
- Add explicit type annotations on HashMap::new() calls used with RwLock
  (required for type inference under the abstracted types)

tests/resize_watchdog_integration.rs:
- Add cfg-gated shutdown helper (same pattern as ipc.rs send_shutdown) for
  asupersync Cx-aware channel sends

tests/runtime_compat_tests.rs:
- Add #![cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))] gate — these tests exercise
  the tokio-specific path and should not compile under asupersync

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
